sanskrit:
उत्क्रामन्तं स्थितं वापि भुञ्जानं वा गुणान्वितम्।
विमूढा नानुपश्यन्ति पश्यन्ति ज्ञानचक्षुषः॥
transliteration:
utkrāmantaḿ sthitaḿ vāpi bhuñjānaḿ vā guṇānvitam |
vimūḍhā nānupaśyanti paśyanti jñāna-cakṣuṣaḥ ||
literal_meaning: The deluded do not perceive the Self when it departs, abides, or enjoys; those with the eye-of-knowledge see.
contextual_meaning: Ordinary awareness is blind to the continuity of consciousness across life-changes; only reflective, trained attention notices the witnessing Self beneath body-states and mood-cycles.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissociation from mortality and the silent observer within; potential for metacognitive awakening.
trigger_scenario: Lying in an MRI machine awaiting results—heart racing, mind jumping to worst-case futures—yet a quiet part notices the whole drama without panic.
anchor_text: Notice the silent witness beneath every thought, feeling, and change.
